input.title: BPC 157 promotes gastric ulcer healing in mice through COX-2 and VEGF upregulation
input.abstract: C57BL/6 mice (n=60) received acetic-acid-induced gastric ulcers and were divided into three groups of 20: BPC 157 (10 µg/kg intraperitoneally, daily), omeprazole (10 mg/kg orally, daily), or saline vehicle for 10 days. Ulcer area on day 10, measured by planimetry, was significantly reduced in BPC 157 mice (2.1 ± 0.4 mm²) vs saline (8.7 ± 1.2 mm²; p<0.001) and comparable to omeprazole (2.4 ± 0.5 mm²). Immunohistochemistry showed elevated COX-2 and VEGF expression in the BPC 157 group. No mortality or abnormal behavior was observed. BPC 157 promotes gastric mucosal healing via pro-angiogenic and anti-inflammatory mechanisms.
